$comment: hands.mcp.v1 — FROZEN 2026-07-09. The tool surface the agent sees, exposed by hands/surface.py as both MCP tools and local HTTP (ADR-058 D4 co-tenant pattern). Tool list ported from the proven prototype (reference/agent.py, 12 tools). Every tool carries an ADR-010 §9 trust tier. Change control per contracts/README.md: additive → v1.1 via PR; breaking → v2 + tell Grant. rev.2 2026-07-12 (additive, GAP_CLOSING_PLAN Phase 0): type_text gains an optional 'target' arg + focus-guard semantics — it refuses (ok:false error:'refused: ...') when the focused window is a terminal, can't be resolved, or doesn't match 'target'. rev.3 2026-07-12 (additive, Phase 1): mouse_click coordinates defined as pixels of the most recent screenshot, auto-mapped to the pointer's logical space (identity when no screenshot on record). rev.4 2026-07-12 (additive, Phase 2): click_element gains the vision-spine fallback (screenshot→local vision model→coordinate click, env WINDYTALK_VISION_URL) for elements accessibility can't see.

tiers.$comment: Enforced by hands/tiers.py at the surface, not by the client or agent. auto_allow: execute immediately. ask_first: per-invocation user confirmation required; the user MAY grant a session-scoped always-allow upgrade for the tool. always_confirm: per-invocation confirmation, no session upgrade possible. Confirmation is delivered through the active client (voice prompt or UI dialog); a denial returns ok:false error:'denied' to the agent — never silence. Tiers are per-tool in v1; per-argument escalation is a v1.1 candidate.

platform_note: The tool list is the stable cross-OS surface. A backend that cannot implement a tool on its platform returns ok:false error:'unsupported' — the tool never disappears from the list.

tools.name: open_app
tools.description: Launch a desktop application by name (e.g. 'Firefox', 'terminal', 'files', 'settings').

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Open the web browser and search the web for a query.

tools.name: open_url
tools.description: Open a specific URL in the default browser.

tools.name: type_text
tools.description: Type text into the currently focused field. Does not press Enter. Focus-guarded: refuses (error 'refused: ...', nothing typed) if the focused window is a terminal, can't be identified, or doesn't match 'target'. Pass 'target' whenever the text is meant for a specific window.

target.description: App name or window-title fragment the text is intended for; typing is refused if the focused window doesn't match it.

tools.name: press_keys
tools.description: Press a key or keyboard shortcut, e.g. 'ctrl+c', 'alt+Tab', 'super', 'Return', 'ctrl+shift+t'.

tools.name: click_element
tools.description: Click an on-screen button, link, or menu item by its visible label. Fast lane = the accessibility tree (semantic, coord-free); when accessibility can't see the element (e.g. Chrome) and a vision model is configured, it falls back to screenshot→vision→coordinate click. Prefer this over mouse_click.

tools.name: mouse_click
tools.description: Click at absolute screen coordinates. Blind (no semantic target) — prefer click_element. Ask-first because a coordinate click can hit anything on screen. Coordinates are pixels of the most recent screenshot (auto-mapped to the screen, incl. Retina 2x); with no screenshot on record they are native screen coordinates.

tools.name: scroll
tools.description: Scroll the active window. Negative = down, positive = up.

tools.name: read_screen
tools.description: Read the visible text and labels of the active window via the accessibility tree (no screenshot needed).

tools.name: list_apps
tools.description: List the currently open, accessible applications.

tools.name: screenshot
tools.description: Capture a screenshot of the whole screen to a PNG file on the local machine. The image never leaves the machine except through the agent's own brain call.

path.description: Destination PNG path; backend default if omitted.

tools.name: run_shell
tools.description: Run a shell command and return its output. Always-confirm: arbitrary execution replaces the prototype's denylist per the §3 ledger — the tier carries the safety weight now. Prefer dedicated tools when one fits.

result_shape.$comment: Every tool returns this shape (as the MCP tool result / HTTP response body, and as voice-session.v1 tool_result fields).

result.description: Human/agent-readable outcome; empty on pure side-effect success.

error.description: Present when ok=false: 'denied' (tier), 'unsupported' (platform), 'timeout', or a failure message.
